title: Fun and Games Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To make something jump up again after it hits the ground.
rounds.explanation: bounce: To make something jump up again after it hits the ground.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To turn a key so a toy can move by itself.
rounds.explanation: wind up: To turn a key so a toy can move by itself.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A small word that shows something is above and touching, like 'on the table'.
rounds.explanation: on: A small word that shows something is above and touching, like 'on the table'.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To move along by turning over and over.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The part at the end of your arm with five fingers.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A small word that shows something is below something else.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
Build the complete model sentence.
Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I wind up my toy car.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: The ball is under the table. Which word tells you where the ball is?
rounds.explanation: Under is the position word; ball and table are naming words and red is a colour.

rounds.prompt: Which word completes the instruction: Touch your ___.
rounds.explanation: You can touch a body part like your nose; jump is an action, fast tells speed and blue is a colour.

rounds.prompt: What is a kind thing to say when you play a game with your friends?
rounds.explanation: Sharing turns is kind and fair; the other sentences would leave a friend out.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What colour was Amal's big ball?
choices: It went up into a big tree
Because he was tall and could shake the branch
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Red

rounds.prompt: What happened to the ball after Bilal threw it?
choices: On a branch of the big tree
It went up into a big tree
rounds.answer: It went up into a big tree
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: It went up into a big tree

rounds.prompt: Why could Ishmael get the ball down when the children could not?
choices: Because he was tall and could shake the branch
On a branch of the big tree
rounds.answer: Because he was tall and could shake the branch
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Because he was tall and could shake the branch

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
rounds.target: Copy an adult's action, then say what you did.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
rounds.target: Show your partner a body part or a toy and tell them about it.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 - Role-play
rounds.target: Play a giving-orders game with a friend.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I touch my shoulder with my hand.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The book is on the table.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The duck swims on the pond.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'feet' mean in this unit?
choices: More than one foot. We stand and walk on our feet.
To make something jump up again after it hits the ground.
To send something through the air with your hand.
rounds.answer: More than one foot. We stand and walk on our feet.
rounds.explanation: feet: More than one foot. We stand and walk on our feet.

rounds.prompt: What does 'left' mean in this unit?
choices: To make something jump up again after it hits the ground.
To send something through the air with your hand.
One side of your body. Your left hand is on that side.
rounds.answer: One side of your body. Your left hand is on that side.
rounds.explanation: left: One side of your body. Your left hand is on that side.

rounds.prompt: What does 'throw' mean in this unit?
choices: To move your legs very fast, faster than walking.
To send something through the air with your hand.
To make something jump up again after it hits the ground.
rounds.answer: To send something through the air with your hand.
rounds.explanation: throw: To send something through the air with your hand.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'head'?
rounds.explanation: head is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'puppy'?
rounds.explanation: puppy is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'shake'?
rounds.explanation: shake is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: To send something through the air with your hand.
To move your legs very fast, faster than walking.
To put your hand or finger on something.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: The part at the end of your leg that you stand on.
The part where your arm joins your body, below your neck.
The long part of your body from your shoulder to your hand.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Two small words that mean beside, right at the side of something.
A bird that swims on water and says quack.
A small flying insect that makes honey and buzzes softly.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Musa throws a ball to Aisha. What should Aisha do with her hands?
rounds.explanation: You catch a ball that comes to you; throwing, dropping and hiding it would end the game.

rounds.prompt: Which word means to move very fast on your legs?
rounds.explanation: Running is fast leg movement; sitting is still, and looking and listening use your eyes and ears.

rounds.prompt: Which one is a question?
rounds.explanation: Only this one asks something and ends with a question mark; the others are telling sentences.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which word is an action word, something you do?
rounds.explanation: Jump is something your body does; a table, rain and a shoe are naming words, not actions.

rounds.prompt: Which action do you do by putting your two hands together?
rounds.explanation: Clapping uses two hands; you run and jump with your legs and blink with your eyes.

rounds.prompt: A ball hits the ground and comes back up. Which word tells what it does?
rounds.explanation: Going down and up again is called bouncing; sleeping, eating and writing are not ball movements.
